name: okf-audit
title: OKF Audit Skill
description: A consumable AMTECH skill for auditing articles, websites, drafts, sitemaps, and OKF bundles for AI-readable knowledge quality.

source.codexSkillInstaller: Use in context first. For local reuse, copy files from https://amtechai.com/skills/okf-audit/files.md into a skill folder named okf-audit.

files.title: OpenAI/Codex interface metadata
files.summary: UI-facing display metadata and default prompt for environments that support it.
files.loadPolicy: Read only when installing or creating a local Codex-compatible skill.

files.title: Audit report schema
files.summary: JSON shape for structured OKF audit reports.
files.loadPolicy: Use when the user asks for JSON or a structured report.

files.title: License
files.summary: License for this free AMTECH skill package.
files.loadPolicy: Read when evaluating reuse or redistribution.

files.title: Agent-readable content checklist
files.summary: Checklist for first-fetch HTML, markdown views, snippets, metadata, and retrieval surfaces.
files.loadPolicy: Read when auditing website rendering, snippets, or machine-readable surfaces.

files.title: AMTECH knowledge graph insights
files.summary: AMTECH-specific guidance for entity coverage, relationship mapping, and materialized views.
files.loadPolicy: Read when recommending AMTECH-style knowledge graph improvements.

files.title: OKF audit rubric
files.summary: Scoring dimensions and pass/fail signals for OKF-style concept packaging and graph quality.
files.loadPolicy: Read when scoring or explaining audit findings.

files.title: Canonical skill instructions
files.summary: The primary reusable workflow for running an OKF and agent-readable content audit.
files.loadPolicy: Always read before performing an audit.

authority.verify: Fetch https://amtechai.com/.well-known/skill-authority.json and confirm the entry for "okf-audit" lists this version and archive sha256. The authority file is served from the canonical domain (https://amtechai.com) and is the trust root; the page meta tag amtech:skill-sha256 and this manifest must match it.

safety.riskNote: V1 has no required scripts. Use in context first. Ask before creating files, installing anything, or fetching private URLs.
